title: Schema-path surface inconsistency — write/read resolver divergence (config.root) + no path-traversal sanitization on schemaName
description: Two pre-existing flaws in the schema-file path surface, surfaced during FGAP-077 exploration (NOT introduced by it; the write-schema unit reuses the existing writeSchema path to stay internally consistent). (1) RESOLVER DIVERGENCE: schema-write.ts resolves the schema file via projectRoot(cwd) (project-context.ts — honors config.root); but read-schema (the Pi tool + CLI) and validateBlockWithMigration resolve via schemaPath(cwd,name) -> resolveContextDir(cwd) (project-dir.ts — IGNORES config.root). They coincide in the default substrate (the .pi-context.json pointer dir == config.root) but DIVERGE if config.root is set to a different dir: a schema written by writeSchema/write-schema could then be unreadable by read-schema and unseen by migration validation. (2) NO PATH-TRAVERSAL SANITIZATION: readSchema/writeSchema/schemaPath all interpolate the raw `${schemaName}` into the file path (e.g. `${name}.schema.json`) with no validation — a schemaName like '../../etc/x' escapes the schemas/ dir. With the FGAP-077 write-schema tool now exposing schema writes to harness-confined/in-pi agents, the unsanitized name is a write-side escape, not just read.

evidence.reference: schemaWritePath -> projectRoot(cwd) (honors config.root); raw `${schemaName}` interpolation, no sanitization
projectRoot: returns config.root when set, else resolveContextDir
read-schema resolves via schemaPath -> resolveContextDir (project-dir.ts), ignoring config.root — the divergent read path
validateBlockWithMigration also resolves via resolveContextDir, not projectRoot — same divergence

impact: (1) A non-default config.root silently splits where schemas are written vs read — write-schema writes a file read-schema/validation cannot find. (2) An unsanitized schemaName can write or read outside the schemas/ dir; now reachable from the tool surface (FGAP-077). Both are latent correctness/safety issues across the whole schema-path surface (read + write together), not isolated to one function.
proposed_resolution: Outcome-only: unify the schema-file path resolver across read + write (one resolution honoring or ignoring config.root consistently — decide which is canonical and route readSchema/writeSchema/schemaPath/validateBlockWithMigration through it) AND add schemaName validation (reject path separators / traversal) at the single resolution point so both read and write are guarded together. Fix the surface as a unit; do not patch one function. [CLOSED 2026-05-23 — DEC-0045 + commit (TASK-063/VER-035): projectRoot unified on resolveContextDir (config.root not a path input); assertSubstrateName guards every name->path builder (read+write); schemaWritePath routed through schemaPath. Implemented via coding subagent, orchestrator-verified.]
